# Coding Agent Instructions for Sinking

## Context
This is a C#/.NET project in early development stages. The project uses Visual Studio solution structure with source code in the `src/` directory.

## Key Guidelines

### Code Organization
- All source code should be placed in the `src/` directory
- Follow standard .NET project structure conventions
- Use appropriate project types for different components
- Maintain clean separation of concerns

### Development Practices
- Write clean, readable C# code following .NET conventions
- Use meaningful names for classes, methods, and variables
- Include appropriate error handling and logging
- Write unit tests for new functionality when applicable

### Dependencies & Packages
- Use NuGet packages for external dependencies
- Prefer official Microsoft packages when available
- Keep package versions up to date with security considerations
- Document any special dependency requirements

### Build & Deployment
- Ensure code builds cleanly with the existing solution file
- Address compiler warnings when possible
- Consider both Debug and Release configurations
- Test changes locally before committing

### Repository Standards
- Follow the existing .gitignore patterns
- Keep commit messages clear and descriptive
- Make focused, atomic commits
- Respect the MIT license terms

## Technology Stack
- **Language**: C#
- **Framework**: .NET
- **Build Tool**: MSBuild/Visual Studio
- **License**: MIT

## Special Considerations
- This project is in early development - prioritize clean, simple implementations
- Consider future extensibility but avoid over-engineering
- Maintain compatibility with standard .NET tooling
- Follow Microsoft's .NET coding guidelines and best practices
